news.title: All eyes on Mbappe, Yamal as Spain, France meet in ‘final before the final’
news.lede: Spain and France rightly feel that the winner of their semi-final will go on to claim the ⁠World Cup. England and Argentina, who play ⁠in the other semi-final in Atlanta on Wednesday (Thursday 3am, Hong Kong time), would disagree ⁠but as Spanish coach Luis de la Fuente said of their showdown in Arlington, Texas: “It’s not an exaggeration to describe this match as a final before the final.” France are the top-ranked team in the Fifa rankings, while Spain are No 3. With Argentina at No 2 and England No 4, this marks the first time the top four teams have reached ‌the semi-finals.

news.title: Beyond the World Cup... The 5️⃣ stories shaping Tuesday
news.lede: The transfer market and preseason preparations are getting underway. We review the five hottest stories in world football that will set the news agenda this summer. José Mourinho begins his new spell at Real Madrid with an eye on the future. The Portuguese coach has called up 16 academy players for the first preseason training session, making it clear he will rely on Madrid’s youth setup.

news.title: World Cup predictions, semifinals: France vs Spain and England vs Argentina
news.lede: Who knows more about soccer? Us ‘experts’, you the subscribers, a child, a dog or an algorithm? Come join us to predict each World Cup game.

news.title: France vs Spain World Cup 2026 semi-final preview: Prediction, team news & line-ups
news.lede: AT&T Stadium sets the stage for a heavyweight 2026 FIFA World Cup semi-final clash between France and Spain. Looking to become only the third team in World Cup history to appear in three consecutive finals, Les Bleus head into proceedings as slim favourites after winning all six matches in regulation time. Ousmane Dembele and World Cup top scorer Kylian Mbappe were on the scoresheet as Didier Deschamps’ side beat Morocco 2-0 in the quarter-finals, booking their tickets for this blockbuster showdown in Arlington, Texas. Unlike their opposition, Spain had to survive late drama to overcome Belgium last time out, with second-half substitute Mikel Merino scoring an 88th-minute winner to seal La Furia Roja’s 2-1 victory.

news.title: World Cup 2026: England and Argentina semi-final to have extra security, say authorities
news.lede: England and Argentina are meeting at the World Cup for the first time since 2002. Authorities say there will be increased security measures in place for Wednesday's World Cup semi-final between England and Argentina. The pair will resume one of international football's most storied rivalries at the Atlanta Stadium, as England bid to defeat the defending champions and reach their first World Cup final since 1966. Atlanta police are aware of historical tensions between the two countries.

news.title: World Cup 2026: Why has England midfielder Kobbie Mainoo failed to play a single minute for Thomas Tuchel's side this summer?
news.lede: Kobbie Mainoo has cut a pretty forlorn-looking figure at this World Cup. He is one of only three outfield players who has not featured at all in the tournament. Ivan Toney and Trevoh Chalobah are the others, and they probably better understand their lot. Chalobah was a late replacement for the injured Tino Livramento and so always knew he was the back-up defender.

news.title: France at the World Cup 2026: Squad, fixtures, group and history
news.lede: Everything you need to know about France at the 2026 World Cup: their fixtures, squad, qualifying record, key players, coach and tournament history. France were runners-up last time and will have an eye on the gold medals at FIFA World Cup 2026. Didier Deschamps' side, led by captain Kylian Mbappé, topped Group I ahead of Norway, Senegal and Iraq to reach the knockout phase. They then beat Sweden in the round of 32, Paraguay in the last 16 and Morocco in the quarter-finals.

news.title: ‘Spain’s superstar fan’: Lamine Yamal’s little brother takes World Cup by storm
news.lede: The Spain winger says three-year-old Keyne ‘means everything to me’ – and fans have taken him to their hearts. He’s become a social media staple; captured on camera blowing kisses, joyfully cheering on his team and careening through the stands of stadiums. Standing at just a few feet tall, the tiny toddler has become the unexpected breakout star of Spain’s World Cup run. “This time around, the biggest sensation of the competition isn’t an athlete, the goals, or even the trophy itself,” was how Hola! – Spain’s version of Hello!
